title: capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. rubella
intro: Capsella bursa-pastoris subsp. rubella, also known as shepherd's purse, is an annual or biennial plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The health benefits of shepherd's purse include its ability to treat menstrual disorders, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. Shepherd's purse can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
